L1.area: HTTP/2 header compression encoding and decoding infrastructure

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the HTTP/2 header compression implementation where variable-length integer decoding performs unchecked arithmetic operations. A remote peer can send a specially crafted HPACK-encoded header block that triggers integer overflow during decoding, causing the process to crash and resulting in a denial of service.

L3.area: HPACK primitive data type encoding and decoding routines within the header compression module
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the HPACK variable-length integer decoding logic where arithmetic operations on accumulated values and bit shifts are performed without overflow checks. A remote HTTP/2 peer can send a specially crafted HPACK-encoded header block containing a malicious variable-length integer encoding that triggers integer overflow during the accumulation step of decoding, causing the process to crash.

ground_truth.reason: The HPACK variable-length integer decoding function uses unchecked multiplication and addition arithmetic that can overflow on crafted inputs, causing a crash instead of a graceful error, enabling denial of service by any HTTP/2 peer.
